sizing_rule.note: Use parametric optimal f (derived from true mathematical probability distribution) rather than empirical optimal f (calculated from limited historical sample). Parametric method accounts for true system probability and avoids underestimation bias from small sample sizes. Example: theoretical 60% win rate → parametric optimal f = 0.2, even if recent sample showed only 55% wins.

source_rule_text: Position size should be based on parametric optimal f derived from the mathematical expectancy of the system rather than empirical data from a limited sample, as parametric optimal f accounts for true probability while empirical optimal f from small samples may underestimate the correct position size.
